plan.step: 1. Schema: add worktree_path column to terminals
plan.change: New migration008 { version: 8, name: 'terminal_worktree', up: db => db.exec(`ALTER TABLE terminals ADD COLUMN worktree_path TEXT`) } modeled exactly on 002_dev_command.ts. Import + append migration008 to the MIGRATIONS array in migrations/index.ts (NEVER edit a shipped migration). This is the persisted per-agent worktree path; cwd column keeps meaning 'main project path / shell start fallback'. Rationale: the renderer spawns PTYs from the DB row (Terminal.tsx reads term.cwd), so the worktree must be reachable from the row, but we keep it in a SEPARATE column so workspace.project_path and terminal.cwd stay = MAIN (preserves the orchestration/memory anchor per findings #10/#11).

plan.step: 2. Types: thread mainProjectPath + worktree through the wire
plan.change: (a) Terminal interface (line 236): add `worktree_path: string | null`. (b) CreateTerminalInput (line 284): add `mainProjectPath?: string` (MAIN tree, for MCP/ORYON_PROJECT_DIR) — distinct from `cwd` (the worktree where the shell runs). (c) TaskStatus union (line 40): add `'blocked'` (needed by reviewer-loop escalation, step 12). (d) FileContent (line 22): add `mtimeMs: number; size: number` (editor conflict guard, step 14). (e) BridgeApi.editor.writeFile signature (line 328): widen to `(path, content, expect?: {mtimeMs:number;size:number}) => Promise<{ok:true}|{ok:false;reason:'diverged';mtimeMs:number;size:number}>`. (f) BridgeApi.source: add `branches: (projectPath:string)=>Promise<Array<{agent:string;branch:string;path:string}>>` and `branchDiff:(projectPath:string,branch:string)=>Promise<SourceDiff[]>` and `mergeAgent:(projectPath:string,branch:string)=>Promise<{ok:boolean;message:string}>` for the worktree-aware Source view (step 9).

Single owner of the git-worktree lifecycle using synchronous execFileSync (setup/teardown run once per swarm start/stop, not hot path; sync = naturally serialized, dodges the .git/worktrees + index.lock race that is the reincarnated #1 blocker). Export: git(cwd,args) wrapper with ['-c','core.quotePath=false', ...args] + windowsHide; isGitRepo(p); worktreeDir(main,agent)=join(main,'.oryon','agents',agent.toLowerCase()) — INSIDE the already-gitignored .oryon/ so the main checkout and the editor watcher (editor.ipc IGNORE_DIRS already has '.oryon') never show 8 trees, and Windows MAX_PATH stays safe (AGENT_NAMES are short); branchFor(agent)=`oryon/agent-${agent.toLowerCase()}`; ensureWorktree(main,agent,base?) — idempotent: resolve baseSha=git(main,['rev-parse', base ?? 'HEAD']); if `worktree list --porcelain` already contains the path AND it exists on disk, return it AS-IS (do NOT auto-reset — a respawn/StrictMode double-mount must reattach existing work, not clobber it; reset only on an explicit fleet-reset action); else `worktree add -B <branch> <dir> <baseSha>` with a catch that runs `worktree prune` then retries (handles stale registration); on non-git main return main (documented shared-cwd fallback). removeWorktree(main,agent) force-removes dir + prunes but LEAVES the branch (agent commits survive). pruneMergedWorktrees(main) removes+deletes branch ONLY when `rev-list --count HEAD..<branch>` === '0' (never lose unmerged work). NOTE: base branch is the current HEAD branch (repo HEAD is 'main' per git rev-parse, despite the stale 'master' in the status snapshot) — resolve dynamically, never hardcode.

plan.step: 4. Provision worktrees where terminals get their cwd (workspace create/addTerminal)
plan.change: Import { isGitRepo, ensureWorktree } from '../services/worktrees'. In workspaces:create (line 45-54 terminal build) set each terminal `worktree_path = isGitRepo(data.projectPath) ? ensureWorktree(data.projectPath, name) : null` and keep `cwd: data.projectPath` (cwd stays MAIN; the renderer will pass cwd=worktree_path||project_path at spawn — see step 7). Add worktree_path to both INSERT statements (create loop line 67-70 and addTerminal line 114-117). In workspaces:addTerminal (line 104-113) do the same single ensureWorktree for the one new agent. All 8 creations happen in ONE synchronous loop at workspace:create → serialized, never concurrent. In workspaces:delete (line 125): before deleting rows, `if (isGitRepo(ws.project_path)) pruneMergedWorktrees(ws.project_path)` and emit a mailbox-style notice for any retained unmerged branch (don't destroy unmerged agent work).

plan.step: 5. Decouple cwd (worktree) from ORYON_PROJECT_DIR + MCP config (MAIN) at terminals:create — THE trap
plan.change: This is the load-bearing memory-sharing fix. Today line 18 `buildProjectMcpConfigForPath(opts.cwd)` would point ORYON_PROJECT_DIR (settings.ipc.ts:82) at whatever cwd is → if cwd becomes the worktree, 8 private memories. Change: `const mcpAnchor = opts.mainProjectPath ?? opts.cwd` and `buildProjectMcpConfigForPath(mcpAnchor)` so ORYON_PROJECT_DIR stays pinned to MAIN regardless of worktree cwd. Keep `ensureClaudeReady(opts.cwd)` on the WORKTREE cwd (it seeds per-path trust in .claude.json keyed by path, claude-launcher.ts:78 — the agent stalls on the trust dialog otherwise). createTerminal still receives cwd: opts.cwd (= the worktree). buildProjectMcpConfigForPath is UNCHANGED — but always fed the MAIN path so projectId resolves (settings.ipc.ts:71) and oryon-mcp-${projectId}.json + project-scoped connectors stay correct (a worktree path has no projects row → would collapse to oryon-mcp-app.json). $0 guard untouched: pty-manager ptyEnv() still strips ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (line 60), opts.env adds only ORYON_* — do NOT introduce any new env path.

plan.step: 6. preload: widen editor.writeFile + add new source bridge methods
plan.change: editor.writeFile (line 36): `(path, content, expect) => ipcRenderer.invoke('editor:writeFile', path, content, expect)`. Add under source (after line in source block): `branches: (p)=>ipcRenderer.invoke('source:branches',p)`, `branchDiff:(p,b)=>ipcRenderer.invoke('source:branchDiff',p,b)`, `mergeAgent:(p,b)=>ipcRenderer.invoke('source:mergeAgent',p,b)`. No change to terminals.create (it already forwards the whole opts object, which now carries mainProjectPath).

plan.step: 7. Renderer: spawn the PTY in the worktree, pass MAIN as mainProjectPath
plan.change: At the create() call (lines 146-154): `cwd: term.worktree_path ?? term.cwd` (shell starts in the per-agent worktree, falling back to main for non-git) and add `mainProjectPath: term.cwd` (term.cwd is the MAIN project_path stored on the row). Net effect with step 5: shell cwd = worktree (code edits + git isolated per agent), ORYON_PROJECT_DIR = MAIN (shared memory). term.worktree_path comes from the DB row threaded through WorkspaceWithTerminals (already available). Also fold in the xterm safeFit guard here — see step 16.

plan.step: 8. Run-file paths → ABSOLUTE into MAIN tree (fixes the silent dispatch break)
plan.change: Add three absolute-path helpers next to tasksDir (which already = <projectPath>/.oryon/run/tasks): `taskFilePath(main,name)=join(tasksDir(main),name)`, `resultFilePath(main,n)=join(tasksDir(main),resultFileName(n))`, `reviewFilePath(main,n)=join(tasksDir(main),reviewFileName(n))`. ALSO fix writeTaskFile dependency rendering (line 91-93): change `tasks/${resultFileName(d)}` to `${resultFilePath(projectPath, d)}` so a dependent agent (cwd=worktree) reads its prerequisites from the MAIN tree, not a nonexistent <worktree>/.oryon path. Update the surrounding heading (line 104-105) to say 'chemins absolus'. ensureGitignore/initRun/tasksDir layout UNCHANGED — the run substrate stays in ONE tree.

plan.step: 9. Router: inject ABSOLUTE main-tree paths into agent prompts; keep ONE watch point
plan.change: Import { join } not needed if using the new helpers; import taskFilePath/resultFilePath/reviewFilePath from run-files. Replace rel()/taskFileRel() (lines 107-113) with absolute builders rooted at swarm.projectPath (the MAIN path, already set in submitGoal/ensureSwarmForWorkspace): `taskFileAbs(num,title)=taskFilePath(swarm!.projectPath, numberToTaskFile.get(num) ?? taskFileName(num,title??''))`. At the 3 dispatch call sites — dispatchReady builder (170-178), dispatchReady reviewer (193-198), runTask (585-592) — pass taskFile/resultFile/reviewFile via the absolute helpers (swarm.projectPath / swarm?.projectPath). Delete rel()/taskFileRel(). Leave startWatcher(ws.project_path) and chokidar.watch(tasksDir(projectPath)) (lines 266-273, 547, 364) EXACTLY as-is — single watch point on MAIN. Dedup keys (processedOutputs/numberToTaskFile, basename in handleOutputFile:208) key on bare filenames → stay correct. Add a guard comment at line 107 and at startWatcher: '// swarm.projectPath = MAIN tree ALWAYS — run dir + watch + absolute agent paths live here, never a worktree.'

plan.step: 10. Roles: prompt wording — absolute paths, split code-edits vs run-files
plan.change: buildAgentPrompt line 126: replace 'relative to the repo root' with 'an ABSOLUTE path — use it verbatim, do not resolve it against your working directory'. Add one sentence: 'Do your CODE edits with normal repo-relative paths in your current working directory (your worktree mirrors the repo); ONLY the task/result/review files use the absolute path above.' Update the AgentPromptOpts JSDoc (lines 112-114) from 'chemin relatif au repo' to 'chemin ABSOLU dans le run du projet principal (identique quel que soit le worktree)'. Same absolute-path wording fix in buildReviewPrompt (lines 150,153); the reviewer's git diff still runs in its worktree cwd, which is correct.

Async git via promisify(execFile) with core.quotePath=false + maxBuffer 64MB (mirror source.ipc). Module-level `let chain: Promise<void> = Promise.resolve()` serializer → exactly one git merge touches MAIN at a time (merge-time analogue of the cwd-isolation race; concurrent merges corrupt MAIN's index). enqueueMergeBack(job) returns chain=chain.then(()=>integrate(job)).catch(()=>{}). integrate(): (1) in the worktree, `add -A` then if `status --porcelain` dirty, `commit -m 'agent <name>: <task>' --no-verify` (machine commit of already-reviewed work; FLAG to owner that --no-verify deviates from the global no-skip-hooks rule, but a swarm merge can't sit on an interactive pre-commit hook); (2) if `rev-list --count HEAD..<branch>` === '0' → onDone('rien à intégrer') and bail; (3) GUARD: require MAIN clean — `diff --quiet` && `diff --cached --quiet`, else onConflict('main a des changements non commités — intégration #N reportée') and return (protects the human's Source-panel/editor session on MAIN — recommended option (a) over a dedicated integration branch); (4) snapshot main via `stash create` (recovery, same pattern as source.ipc snapshotRecovery); (5) `merge --no-ff -m '...' <branch>`; on failure `merge --abort` + onConflict with the exact `git stash apply <snap>` recovery line and 'branche CONSERVÉE pour merge manuel'. Agent work is NEVER destroyed — it lives on the branch.

plan.step: 12. Router: hook merge-back on approved, cap reviewer loop, escalate to blocked
plan.change: (a) Add `const reviewRounds = new Map<number,number>()` near the other maps (line 56) + `const MAX_REVIEW_ROUNDS = 3`; clear() it in initSwarmState (line 341-344). (b) In dispatchReady builder branch, after assigning num, `if (!reviewRounds.has(num)) reviewRounds.set(num, 0)`. (c) Rewrite the review branch (lines 255-261): if status==='approved' → enqueueMergeBack({ mainPath: swarm.projectPath, worktree: worktreeDir(swarm.projectPath, terminalName(task.assigned_terminal_id)), branch: branchFor(name), agent: name, task: String(parsed.n), onConflict: m=>notice(...), onDone: m=>notice(...) }), updateTask(complete), reviewRounds.delete(parsed.n). Else if status==='changes' → increment round; if >=MAX_REVIEW_ROUNDS notice escalation + updateTask(status:'blocked') (terminal, stops the infinite builder↔reviewer ping-pong that pins 2 terminals) else processedOutputs.delete(resultFileName) + updateTask(todo). Else (UNKNOWN verdict — parseOutputFile is permissive, run-files.ts:127 matches any STATUS word) → notice + updateTask(status:'blocked') (do NOT loop forever on a typo'd 'STATUS: needs-work'). Import worktreeDir/branchFor from worktrees, enqueueMergeBack from merge-back.

plan.step: 13. Router: interrupt (ESC) orphaned agents before requeue/stop; raise silence budget
plan.change: Add `const interrupting = new Set<string>()` and an async interruptTerminal(id): if hasLiveTerminal, add to interrupting, writeTerminal(id,'\x1b'), wait 120ms, writeTerminal(id,'\x1b') (double-ESC: claude TUI sometimes needs two to break a running turn), then poll terminalLastData until ~600ms of silence or 3s cap, then delete from interrupting. Gate pickFreeTerminal (line 91) and runBroadcast's free filter (line 391) with `&& !interrupting.has(id)` so a still-interrupting PTY isn't reused (prevents 2 agents on 1 task — now 2 worktrees + duplicate merges). Call interruptTerminal: watchdogTick before requeue (line 308-313); stopSwarm for every busy terminal before clearing (lines 611-617); reviewer-cap escalation (step 12). Do NOT ESC the changes/blocked self-finished paths (the agent already wrote result.md and is idle). Raise SILENCE_TIMEOUT (line 34) 240000 → 600000 (Opus 'ultracode' builders routinely pause >4 min between stdout flushes → false-positive orphaning).

plan.step: 14. Editor IPC: atomic write + optimistic-concurrency conflict guard
plan.change: readFile (line 47-53): return mtimeMs+size from the stat already taken. writeFile (line 55-57): accept optional `expect?:{mtimeMs;size}`; if provided and current stat diverges (mtimeMs!==expect.mtimeMs || size!==expect.size) return {ok:false, reason:'diverged', mtimeMs, size} (an agent/merge-back rewrote it since open); else write atomically via temp+rename in the same dir (mirrors claude-launcher.ts:94 trusted pattern; prevents truncation visible to a concurrent agent read and the chokidar race) and return {ok:true}. Under worktrees the human's MAIN editor is mostly insulated from live agent edits (they're in worktrees) — the real danger window is the merge-back mutating MAIN, so the step-11 clean-main guard is the primary protection; this hardens the residual race.

plan.step: 15. Editor renderer: store version, prompt on divergence, banner on external change
plan.change: OpenFile interface (line 11-17): add mtimeMs/size. openFile (line 55): destructure + store them from readFile. save() (line 83-94): pass {mtimeMs,size} to writeFile; on {ok:false} window.confirm('<name> a été modifié sur disque (probablement par un agent)… Écraser ?') — if no, re-read and replace buffer (dirty:false); if yes, writeFile without expect (explicit override); after a successful write, refresh mtimeMs/size (follow-up readFile) so the next save compares against the just-written version. FS-event reload (lines 163-171): when an external change hits a DIRTY file, don't silently keep stale — toast '<name> modifié par un agent — recharge pour voir ses changements' and refresh mtimeMs/size so the next save's divergence check fires.

plan.step: 16. Terminal renderer: guard fit()/resize against 0-size/detached panes
plan.change: Add canFit() = el.isConnected && el.offsetParent!==null && clientWidth>0 && clientHeight>0, and safeFit() that returns early if !canFit(), checks fit.proposeDimensions() yields finite cols>=2/rows>=1, then fit.fit() in a try/catch. Replace fit.fit() in the mount rAF (line 141) and the ResizeObserver (line 159) with safeFit(); in the RO only call terminals.resize when canFit() && created. Kills the recurring 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading dimensions)' the 8-pane 'eight' layout triggers when panes mount hidden / collapse to 0 — and stops the bogus PTY resize. PTY still spawns with xterm defaults (80x24) when not yet fittable; the RO corrects it once visible.

plan.step: 17. Source IPC: scope acceptAll, add worktree/branch-aware views + merge action
plan.change: (a) acceptAll (lines 239-241): replace `git add -A` with staging ONLY the gitStatus()-listed files, pathspec-explicit, skipping .oryon/, batched 200 at a time, staging both rename source+dest (consistent with the global 'stage by path, never add -A/.' rule; avoids staging other agents' in-flight work + .oryon trash in the shared MAIN tree). (b) New source:branches(main) → parse `worktree list --porcelain`, map registered .oryon/agents/<name> dirs to {agent, branch=oryon/agent-<name>, path}. (c) source:branchDiff(main,branch) → per-file diffs from `diff --name-status <base>...<branch>` then reuse gitDiff per file (range diff = the per-agent review surface, since agent edits are committed on branches, not in MAIN's working tree → source:status on MAIN would otherwise show nothing). (d) source:mergeAgent(main,branch) → delegate to the same serialized integrate() path from merge-back (NOT a parallel git add -A) so manual merges are also conflict-safe and serialized. Register all three in registerSourceIpc().

worktreePathDecision: Worktrees live INSIDE the main repo under the already-gitignored .oryon/, one per agent: <main>/.oryon/agents/<agent-name-lowercase> (e.g. .oryon/agents/nell). Branch per agent: oryon/agent-<name>. Rationale: (1) .oryon/ is already in IGNORE_DIRS for the editor watcher (editor.ipc.ts:7-10) and gitignored by ensureGitignore (run-files.ts:57), so the 8 worktree checkouts never pollute the file tree, the Source panel, or chokidar; git tracks worktrees via <main>/.git/worktrees regardless of .gitignore, so registration is unaffected. (2) Short AGENT_NAMES keep paths well under Windows MAX_PATH 260 — a sibling like <main>/../.oryon-wt risks colliding with the very noisy parent C:\\Users\\Kolyxe (per the git-status snapshot) and complicates path resolution. (3) The chokidar run-watch is scoped to <main>/.oryon/run/tasks, NOT all of .oryon, so the agents/ subdir is never watched. CRITICAL invariant that survives this choice: cwd = worktree (isolation for code edits + git diff), but ORYON_PROJECT_DIR = MAIN and ALL task/result/review paths = ABSOLUTE into MAIN's .oryon/run — memory stays shared and there is exactly ONE chokidar watch point on the MAIN tree. workspace.project_path and the terminals.cwd column stay = MAIN; the worktree lives only in the new terminals.worktree_path column. Non-git projects fall back to cwd=main (shared cwd, documented).
mergeStrategy:
Integrator-driven, serialized, conflict-surfacing — NOT git add -A on MAIN. Each agent commits to its own branch oryon/agent-<name> inside its worktree. On reviewer 'approved' (router.ts review branch), enqueue a merge-back job onto a single module-level promise chain (merge-back.ts) so exactly ONE `git merge` mutates MAIN at a time — concurrent merges would corrupt MAIN's index (the cwd-isolation race reincarnated at merge time). integrate() per job: (1) commit the worktree's pending edits (--no-verify machine commit — flagged to owner as a deliberate deviation from the no-skip-hooks rule); (2) skip if the branch is 0 commits ahead of HEAD; (3) REQUIRE MAIN clean (diff --quiet + diff --cached --quiet) else defer with a mailbox notice — protects the human's editor/Source session on MAIN; (4) snapshot MAIN (stash create, logged for recovery); (5) merge --no-ff; on conflict: merge --abort, KEEP the branch, post the exact `git stash apply <sha>` recovery line to the mailbox. Agent work is never destroyed (always recoverable from the branch). Manual merges via source:mergeAgent route through the same serialized integrate(). Teardown (workspaces:delete + explicit fleet-reset) removes + deletes ONLY fully-merged worktree branches (rev-list HEAD..branch === 0); unmerged branches are retained and surfaced in a mailbox notice for hand-merge. Default stopSwarm does NOT remove worktrees (a stop is often transient / workspace-switch — removing would lose uncommitted agent work)."
}
risks: Memory-sharing trap (the #1 risk): if step 5 is missed and terminals:create keeps deriving the MCP config from opts.cwd, pointing cwd at the worktree silently splits ORYON_PROJECT_DIR into 8 private .oryon/memory dirs — the whole point of the run is lost, with no error. Verify after submit that the oryon MCP server env carries the MAIN path and that only ONE .oryon/run/tasks receives files.

Dispatch break (the #2 silent failure): if run-file paths injected into prompts stay relative (router rel()), agents with cwd=worktree write <worktree>/.oryon/run/... which the single MAIN chokidar never sees → every task hangs to SILENCE_TIMEOUT then bounces to todo forever, zero forward progress. Steps 8-10 must land together; verify a 2-task goal with dependsOn lists an absolute path that exists on disk in MAIN.

Self-hosting recursion: the worktrees check out THIS repo, so each worktree contains its own src/ — fine and intended (isolation), but a merge-back commits Oryon's own code; the human must keep MAIN clean during a run or merges defer. Confirm the clean-main guard fires before the first merge.

--no-verify on agent commits deviates from the global git rule (CLAUDE.md). It's required so a swarm merge can't sit on an interactive pre-commit hook, but the owner must explicitly accept it; alternative is to disable hooks for worktrees.

git worktree concurrency: ensureWorktree MUST run serially (synchronous execFileSync in the single workspace:create loop). If ever called from the per-terminal renderer spawn path in parallel, 8 calls race on .git/worktrees/index.lock. Keep it out of the hot path.

Base-branch ambiguity: the git-status snapshot says 'master' but `git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD` says 'main'. ensureWorktree must resolve the base branch dynamically from HEAD, never hardcode, or worktree add forks from the wrong ref.

Orphaned-agent ESC timing: interruptTerminal is async and gates pickFreeTerminal via the interrupting set; if the gate is forgotten at any requeue site, a still-running agent's terminal looks free and a second agent piles onto the same task → duplicate worktree commits → duplicate/garbage merges. All three requeue sites (watchdog, stopSwarm, reviewer-cap) must gate.

Migration safety: 008 must be appended, never inserted; editing a shipped migration re-runs on existing DBs. ALTER ADD COLUMN worktree_path is nullable so existing terminals rows degrade to cwd=main fallback.

Editor conflict guard changes the writeFile return type (void → object); every caller (currently only EditorPanel.save) must handle the new shape or saves silently break. Run pnpm typecheck before commit (TS).

Stale worktree dir deleted out-of-band: a stored worktree_path may point at a missing dir on respawn → pty.spawn throws (pty-manager has no cwd-existence guard). Mitigate by re-running idempotent ensureWorktree at spawn or guarding cwd existence with a fallback to main.
summary: Ordered 17-step plan delivering git-worktree-per-agent isolation plus the 5 isolation-hardening highs, grounded in the actual code (router.ts, terminals.ipc.ts, run-files.ts, roles.ts, settings.ipc.ts, workspaces.ipc.ts, pty-manager.ts, source.ipc.ts, editor.ipc.ts, Terminal.tsx, EditorPanel.tsx, types.ts, migrations/index.ts). The spine is decoupling three axes that today all collapse from one value (project_path): cwd → per-agent worktree under <main>/.oryon/agents/<name> (code-edit + git isolation), ORYON_PROJECT_DIR → MAIN (shared memory, never split), and task/result/review paths → ABSOLUTE into MAIN's .oryon/run (one chokidar watch point, dispatch never hangs). Sequencing rationale: schema+types first (1-2) so every later edit compiles; the worktree service (3) before its wirings (4-7); the routing trio (8-10) must land together or dispatch silently breaks; merge-back service (11) before its router hook (12); then the agent-isolation highs (13 ESC/silence, 14-15 editor clobber, 16 xterm, 17 source acceptAll + worktree-aware diff/merge). Merge-back is integrator-driven and serialized via a single promise chain with a clean-MAIN guard, snapshot, and conflict-abort-and-preserve-branch — never git add -A, never lose agent work. The two critical traps to not miss: the MCP/memory anchor (step 5) and the absolute run-file paths (steps 8-10). One open item for the owner: --no-verify on machine merge-back commits deviates from the global no-skip-hooks rule and needs explicit sign-off."}
